examples.title: Algorithm: Android Build Process Workflow
examples.description: Demonstrates the sequence of operations during Android project build
examples.code:
ALGORITHM AndroidBuildProcess
BEGIN
 1. Validate project structure and Gradle configuration
 2. Resolve dependencies from repositories
 3. Compile Java/Kotlin source code to bytecode
 4. Process Android resources (res/ directory)
 5. Generate R.java and BuildConfig.java
 6. Convert bytecode to DEX format
 7. Package resources and code into APK
 8. Sign APK with debug/release keys
END
examples.explanation: This workflow shows how the directory structure components (java/, res/, build.gradle) interact during build. Steps 4-5 directly use res/ directory contents, while step 2 uses build.gradle dependencies.

examples.title: Algorithm: Resource Merging Process
examples.description: Shows how Android merges resources from different directories
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MergeResources
BEGIN
 1. Collect all res/ directories (main, flavors, libraries)
 2. Prioritize resources by source:
 a. Build variants highest priority
 b. Main resources medium
 c. Library resources lowest
 3. Resolve conflicting resource names
 4. Generate merged res/ structure in build/intermediates
 5. Validate against AndroidManifest.xml requirements
END
examples.explanation: Explains how the res/ directory structure affects final app resources. Step 3 handles cases where multiple res/ directories define same resource name.

examples.title: Algorithm: Dependency Resolution Process
examples.description: Illustrates Gradle's dependency resolution using build.gradle
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ResolveDependencies
BEGIN
 1. Parse dependencies block in build.gradle
 2. Check local Gradle cache
 3. IF cached version exists:
 a. Use local artifact
 4. ELSE:
 a. Query repositories in declared order
 b. Download from first available source
 c. Store in cache
 5. Resolve version conflicts using:
 a. Force directives
 b. Newest version
 c. Dependency tree
END
examples.explanation: Relates to build.gradle file structure. Step 4b uses repositories defined in project-level build.gradle. Step 5 handles version conflicts common in multi-module projects.
